name: Administration of Resources and Choices (Arc)
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor and elder protection services organization in Arizona, offering free homebuyer education, mortgage counseling, and financial assistance for seniors.
description_long:
A.R.C. (Administration of Resources and Choices) is a nonprofit organization based in Arizona that provides HUD-approved housing counseling and elder protection services. Founded with a mission to help Tucsonans achieve stable housing and age safely in their communities, the organization operates as a free resource for residents seeking guidance on homeownership and financial security in later life.

The organization offers multiple core services centered on housing access and senior financial protection. Their HUD-Certified Housing Counselors (averaging 10+ years of experience) provide personalized guidance through the homebuying process, including financial readiness assessments, mortgage pre-qualification coaching, and down payment assistance information (up to $40,000 available). They also counsel distressed homeowners, help seniors evaluate reverse mortgage options, and provide elder protection services including fraud prevention and financial recovery assistance after financial exploitation.

A.R.C. distinguishes itself through its integrated approach to housing security across the lifespan—serving first-time homebuyers, struggling homeowners, and aging adults. Their counselors are HUD-certified professionals rather than sales representatives, positioning financial guidance as education rather than product sales. The organization operates as a community-based nonprofit sustained by donations, volunteers, and partnerships rather than commission-based revenue, eliminating conflicts of interest common in for-profit financial services.

The main caveat is that A.R.C. operates primarily in Arizona (specifically highlighted for Tucson), limiting geographic accessibility. While their services are genuinely free and expert-led, they function as a counseling and referral organization rather than a direct lender or financial product provider. Consumers must still qualify for down payment assistance and mortgage products through partner programs, and the organization's capacity may be limited by nonprofit resources.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors with average 10+ years of professional experience
Completely free services funded by donations and nonprofit model eliminates sales commissions
Offers up to $40,000 down payment assistance for qualifying first-time homebuyers
Provides elder fraud protection and financial recovery services, including legal protection assistance
Services span entire homeownership lifecycle—from first-time buyer prep through distressed homeowner support to reverse mortgage counseling
Free reverse mortgage counseling specifically for seniors considering this option
Community volunteer program allows residents to contribute to mission-driven work
cons: Geographically limited to Arizona (primarily Tucson area), not accessible nationally
As a counseling organization, cannot directly provide loans or guarantee down payment assistance approval
Limited information on website about response times, availability, or waitlists for counselor appointments
Down payment assistance eligibility criteria not fully detailed on website—consumers must contact to determine qualification
No online counseling platform mentioned; services appear to require in-person or phone consultation
best_for: Arizona residents (especially Tucson area) planning to purchase their first home and seeking expert, free guidance
Seniors concerned about reverse mortgage options and needing objective counseling before committing
Homeowners facing financial distress or mortgage delinquency who need non-judgmental counseling
Older adults who may be vulnerable to financial exploitation and need elder protection services
diagnosis: A.R.C. is best for Arizona residents seeking objective, expert housing counseling at no cost, particularly first-time homebuyers and seniors. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Arizona and lack of direct lending capability—they educate and guide toward products rather than provide them directly.
services: HUD-certified homebuyer readiness and financial counseling
First-time homebuyer education and mortgage pre-qualification coaching
Down payment assistance program information and application support (up to $40,000)
Reverse mortgage counseling and education for seniors
Distressed homeowner counseling and mortgage delinquency assistance
Elder protection services including fraud victim financial recovery
Legal protection assistance for seniors experiencing financial exploitation
Housing stability consultation for aging in place
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3003 N Central Ave Ste 610, Phoenix, AZ 85012
meta_description: HUD-certified non-profit in Arizona offering free homebuyer education, mortgage counseling, and financial assistance for seniors.
